questions.q: How many players from each team are on the field during a standard football/soccer match?
questions.explanation: Each team has 11 players on the field during a standard football match: 1 goalkeeper, 4 defenders, 4 midfielders, and 2 forwards (though formations vary). Having 10 or 12 would result in an illegal match setup.

questions.q: What is the length of a standard football field?
questions.explanation: A standard football field is 100-130 yards long (also expressed as 100-110 meters). The width must be 50-100 yards, giving players and teams slightly different field dimensions to work with at different venues.

questions.q: In which year was the first FIFA World Cup held?
questions.explanation: The first FIFA World Cup was held in Uruguay in 1930, with 13 teams competing. This tournament established the tradition of the World Cup being held every four years, though World War II caused a break from 1942-1946.

questions.q: How long is a standard football match?
questions.explanation: A standard football match lasts 90 minutes, divided into two halves of 45 minutes each with a halftime break. Extra time of 30 minutes may be added in knockout stages if the match is tied.

questions.q: What is the term for an indirect kick in football?
options: A free kick from which you cannot score directly
questions.explanation: An indirect free kick is a kick from which the ball must touch another player before a goal can be scored. Direct free kicks allow a goal to be scored directly from the kick, typically awarded for more serious fouls.

questions.q: Which country has won the most FIFA World Cup titles historically?
questions.explanation: Brazil has won the most FIFA World Cup titles with five championships (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002). Germany and Italy have each won four titles, while France has won two World Cups.

questions.q: What does a yellow card signify in football?
options: A warning for foul play or misconduct
An automatic ejection from the game
questions.explanation: A yellow card is a caution or warning for unsporting behavior, fouls, or misconduct. Two yellow cards in the same match result in a red card and ejection. A red card is an automatic ejection.

questions.q: What is the area in front of the goal where the goalkeeper has special privileges called?
options: Both the penalty area and goal area
questions.explanation: Both terms are correct: the larger rectangular zone is called the penalty area or penalty box (18 yards from the goal line), and the smaller box directly in front of the goal is the goal area. The goalkeeper can handle the ball with hands only within this region.

questions.q: How many substitutes can be made during a standard football match?
questions.explanation: Teams are typically allowed three substitutions during a standard football match. In some competitions or unusual circumstances (such as injury recovery periods), additional substitutions may be permitted.

questions.q: What is the role of a midfielder in football?
options: Linking defense and attack, supporting both
Standing in the center circle without moving
questions.explanation: Midfielders link the defense and attack, providing support for both. They control the flow of the game, distribute the ball, and contribute to both defensive and offensive plays, making them crucial to a team's balance.
